LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS BRENNAN MATTHEW KALL AND CHRISTOPHER KALL'S MOTION TO COMPEL MENTAL EXAMINATION IS OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS NOTICED THEIR MOTION FOR HEARING ON 5/19/2026. ON 4/27/2026 AND BY WRITTEN ORDER, THE COURT, THE HONORABLE RICHARD C. DARWIN PRESIDING, WITHOUT CONDUCTING A HEARING GRANTED DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS EX PARTE TO SHORTEN TIME AND ADVANCE THE HEARING TO 5/14/2026. THE COURT ALSO ADVANCED THE DATE FOR ANY OPPOSITION TO MAY 5, 2026. DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS, HOWEVER, FAILED TO FILE A NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE 4/27/2026 ORDER OR OTHERWISE PROVE THEY GAVE NOTICE OF THE ORDER TO THE OTHER PARTIES. THERE IS NO RECORD THE OTHER PARTIES WAIVED NOTICE. ON THIS RECORD, PROCEEDING ACCORDING TO THE 4/27/2026 ORDER WOULD VIOLATE FUNDAMENTAL TENETS OF DUE PROCESS. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Saleem Belbahri, Esq. 628-253-5070 Mansfield Rule for Defendant.
Court Reporter: Marco Neilly, CSR #13564, 415-591-3333, neillymarco@gmail.com
No Other Appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

(see word miniutes for full details)

Moving Defendants are ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

The Court signs two orders.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mental Examination By Defendants/Cross-Complainants Brennan Matthew Kall And Christopher Kall
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Advanced From May-19-26 To May-14-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mental Examination By Defendants/Cross-Complainants Brennan Matthew Kall And Christopher Kall
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRENNAN KALL's Motion To Compel Mental Examination By Defendants/Cross-Complainants Brennan Matthew Kall And Christopher Kall.

Defendants/Cross-Complainants Brennan Matthew Kall And Christopher Kall's Motion To Compel Mental Examination is OFF CALENDAR.

Defendants/Cross-Complainants noticed their motion for hearing on 5/19/2026. On 4/27/2026 and by written order, the court, the Honorable Richard C. Darwin presiding, without conducting a hearing granted Defendants/Cross-Complainants ex parte to shorten time and advance the hearing to 5/14/2026. The court also advanced the date for any opposition to May 5, 2026. Defendants/Cross-Complainants, however, failed to file a notice of entry of the 4/27/2026 order or otherwise prove they gave notice of the order to the other parties. There is no record the other parties waived notice. On this record, proceeding according to the 4/27/2026 order would violate fundamental tenets of due process.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-13-2026 Continued To Jun-22-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-28-2025 Continued To Mar-09-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Initial
Verfications To Propouded Discovery, Further Verified
Supplemental Responses To Set One Of Form And Special
Interrogatories, And Request For Sactions
Off Calendar Oct-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. DAVID MARCOLINI, Esq. (tel: 415-568-0742) specially appearing for Plaintiff Christina Regina Elisa De Simone.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MARK DEVITO's Motion To Compel Initial Verifications To Propounded Discovery, Further Verified Supplemental Responses To Set One Of Form And Special Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions.

Matter discussed. The Court notes it has not received notice of contest by the Plaintiff. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is granted in part. It appears that verified responses have now been provided, and as such, that part of the motion is now moot. As to monetary sanctions, they are awarded in favor of Defendant Devito and against Plaintiff and her counsel, jointly and severally, in the amount of $450 (reduced from the requested amount). The monetary sanctions of $450 shall be due and payable by check delivered to the office of Defendant's counsel within fourteen (14) calendar days of notice of entry of order.

Counsel for the prevailig party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312, and email to the Judge Pro Tem.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
